my_thoughts: The comparison_contrast mechanism with UM_05 feels like unknown is being met with a specific geometric cut. But what if the geometry itself is what they're resisting? The conceptual_inquiry, expecting_bliss suggests they're aware of something but can't name it.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student says 'This change -- you call it a `calamity'?'—unknown via conceptual inquiry, expecting bliss. Teacher responds '' using comparison contrast to redefines enlightenment, compares expectation to reality, warns student. Opening UNMAKING move addresses unknown. Predicting resist intellectually because comparison contrast interventions typically produce this response.
visible_response:
You see, people usually imagine that so-called enlightenment... is something ecstatic, that you will be permanently happy, in a blissful state all the time... But when this kind of a thing happens to the individual, he realizes that really there is no basis for that kind of thing. ... So, from the point of view of the man who 
imagines that to be something permanent, this is a calamity because he is expecting something whereas what happens is altogether unrelated to that.

That's why I very often tell people "If I could give you some glimpse of what this is all about, you wouldn't touch this with a barge pole, a ten foot pole." You would run away from this because this is not what you want.
